steps.title: Compute the boundary list
steps.content: All answer choices use $x=4$, $6$, and $8$. Compute the right side of $y\lt5x+4$ for each value: $$5(4)+4=24$$ $$5(6)+4=34$$ $$5(8)+4=44.$$ The $y$-values must be less than $24$, $34$, and $44$, respectively.

steps.title: Compare the candidate to the boundary
steps.content: The correct table has $x$-values $4$, $6$, and $8$ paired with $y$-values $20$, $30$, and $40$. Each output is strictly below the matching cutoff: $20\lt24$, $30\lt34$, and $40\lt44$.

steps.title: Eliminate the failed choices
steps.content: The boundary table with $y$-values $24$, $34$, and $44$ is not strictly below the cutoff. Another table fails because $38$ is above the allowed cutoff $34$, and the remaining table fails immediately at $(4,28)$. The table with all three $y$-values below their cutoffs is correct, so C is correct.
